mnemonics: CIA: Convex sets are like an Intelligence Agency's safe house. Any point between two agents (points) inside is also safe (inside the set).
JENSEN'S INEQUALITY: Just Expect Non-linear Smooth Effects, Now Interpolate. Only Convex functions Underestimate linear Interpolations, Valuing Averages.
keyPoints: A set is convex if the line segment between any two points in the set lies entirely within the set.
A function is convex if the line segment between any two points on its graph lies on or above the graph itself.
A separating hyperplane exists between two disjoint convex sets, providing a linear inequality that divides the space between them.
commonMistakes: Assuming all functions with a single minimum are convex (e.g., f(x)=x^4 is convex, but f(x)=x^3 is not).
Assuming that if a hyperplane can be drawn between two sets, they are convex (the hyperplane theorem requires the sets to be convex and disjoint).
